atp_cand_hero:
Republican for County Commissioner • Precinct 4, Rockwall County, Texas

Proven Leadership. Real Results.

John Stacy is a lifelong Rockwall County resident, Army veteran, and small business owner running to bring fiscal accountability and common-sense leadership to the Commissioner's Court.

Support This Campaign →
atp_cand_about:
About the Candidate

Meet John Stacy

John Stacy grew up on a family ranch in eastern Rockwall County. After graduating from Rockwall High School, he enlisted in the U.S. Army and served two tours overseas. He earned his degree in Public Administration from Texas A&M and returned home to build Stacy Construction, which has employed over 200 local workers in the last 15 years.

As a father of three and active member of First Baptist Church, John has served on the Rockwall ISD facilities committee, coached Little League for 8 years, and chaired the county's infrastructure advisory board. He believes government should be run like a well-managed business — transparent budgets, measurable outcomes, and respect for taxpayers.

Rockwall County is one of the fastest-growing counties in Texas, but our infrastructure hasn't kept pace. Roads are crumbling, development fees aren't covering the true cost of growth, and the current commissioner hasn't held a public town hall in three years. John is running because taxpayers deserve a commissioner who shows up, listens, and gets results.

Profession
President, Stacy Construction LLC

atp_cand_issues:
Where I Stand

Key Issues

John is the only candidate with both military discipline and construction industry expertise — he knows how to manage budgets, build infrastructure, and lead teams under pressure.

Priority Issue

Infrastructure & Transportation

Rockwall County has added 18,000 residents in 5 years but hasn't expanded a single major road. I'll prioritize the FM 552 corridor widening, the Horizon Road interchange, and a county-wide road condition assessment in my first 90 days. We'll use impact fees from new development to fund improvements — growth should pay for itself.

Priority Issue

Taxes & Government Spending

I'll publish a quarterly budget dashboard so every taxpayer can see where their money goes. I oppose a property tax increase and will find savings through competitive bidding, contract audits, and eliminating redundant county positions.

Priority Issue

Public Safety & Crime

Our sheriff's department is understaffed for a county our size. I'll support hiring 12 additional deputies and expanding the mental health crisis response team. Safe communities attract businesses and keep property values strong.

Priority Issue

Veterans & Military

Rockwall County has over 4,000 veterans but no dedicated county veteran services coordinator. I'll create that position and partner with the VA North Texas system to bring mobile health clinics to our community.

Priority Issue

Housing & Development

Growth is good — but only if it's managed. I'll update the county's land use plan for the first time in 12 years, require developers to fund infrastructure concurrent with construction, and protect agricultural land from unchecked sprawl.
